cohort.hint: Contacts grouped by their creation month. Shows how many progressed to active and how many closed (won/lost).

import.title: Import contacts
import.subtitle: Upload a CSV, review the preview and confirm the import.

import.formatRequired: Only «name» is required. Other columns are optional.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Your profile data, preferences and appearance.
settings.emailHint: Not editable here. Contact an admin to change.
settings.roleHint: Only a group admin can change this.
settings.defaultPillarHint: Pillar where you land by default on entry.

errors.avatarType: Invalid format. Use PNG, JPG, WEBP or GIF.
errors.avatarTooLarge: Image larger than 1 MB. Compress or use another.

gdpr.title: Export my data (GDPR)
gdpr.hint: Download a JSON containing your profile, entitlements, notifications, logged hours and every audit log entry where you appear (as actor or target). Group data (contacts, events, etc.) is not included.

calendarSync.title: Calendar sync
calendarSync.subtitle: Import events from Google Calendar or Outlook into the backoffice. One-way for now — local edits are never overwritten.
calendarSync.note: Manual sync. Range: 7 days back to 90 days forward. Requires OAuth credentials in Vercel — see INTEGRATIONS.md.
calendarSync.notConfigured: OAuth is not configured on this install. See INTEGRATIONS.md.
calendarSync.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect {label}? Already imported events stay, but stop syncing.
calendarSync.pillarHint: Required in order to sync. Imported events default to this pillar.

theme.hint: Applies to this browser. Default follows your system.

library.title: Library
library.subtitle: Living catalog of Backpackers activities — briefing, costs, flow, target audience. Reference before proposing.
library.emptyDescription: As soon as a super user creates the first activity, it will appear here.

landing.title: Administration
landing.subtitle: Manage users, templates and custom fields.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Manage roles and pillar access.
users.count: {count, plural, =0 {No users} one {1 user} other {# users}}
users.roleHint: Group admin sees and edits everything. Pillar admin manages only assigned pillars. Member has read access in assigned pillars.
users.pillarAccessHint: For pillar admin and member: pillars they can access. Ignored for group admin (sees all).
users.targetSuperUserLocked: This user is a super user. Only another super user can edit them.

disable.confirmDisableHint: History and ownership of past records are preserved. The user loses backoffice access until re-enabled.

skills.hint: Each skill grants access to one module. Super user has them all implicitly.
skills.superUserNote: This user is a super user — has all skills automatically, even if unchecked.

descriptions.crm: View and manage contacts, leads, customers, pipeline.
descriptions.ops: View and manage events, projects, tasks, calendar.
descriptions.admin: Manage users, roles, skills and pillar access.

apps.title: Apps & Access
apps.cardSubtitle: Catalog of Backpackers apps and who has access.
apps.landingSubtitle: Each app manages its own access list. Click an app to grant/revoke users.
apps.noAppsYet: No apps in the catalog yet. Add the first one below.
apps.newSectionHint: Each app represents a Backpackers product (e.g. Cairn Pro, another SaaS) you grant access to.
apps.appSubtitleFallback: {active, number} with access · {total, number} users in the system.
apps.autoAccessHint: Super user — automatic access to every app, no manual grant needed.

metadata.title: App metadata
metadata.hint: Name, icon and public URL. The URL is used by the «My apps» shortcuts in the sidebar.
metadata.name: Name

apps.enforcementNote: Access is enforced at the external app's login and on the AI proxy (server-side) via has_app_access(uid, app_key). Set «no access» to lock a user out.

catalog.title: Activity catalog
catalog.subtitle: The group's living library — Backpackers activities with costs, flow and operational briefing. Editable by super users.
catalog.empty: No activities in the catalog yet.
catalog.emptyDescription: Create the first activity — name, family, duration, pax, prices and briefing.
catalog.deleteConfirm: Delete the activity "{name}" from the catalog? Existing deals are not affected.

form.name: Name
form.bodyHint: Minute-by-minute flow, equipment, risks, adaptations — in markdown. Shown in the library for reference.

customFields.title: Custom fields
customFields.subtitle: Define extra fields per entity (contacts, events, projects, deals).
customFields.deleteConfirm: Delete the field "{name}"? Saved values on existing records stay in the database but become non-editable.
customFields.editHint: Entity, key and type are immutable. Changing them requires creating a new field.

workflows.title: Automations
workflows.subtitle: Trigger → conditions → actions rules. Run inline whenever the backoffice mutates the source entity.
workflows.newHint: Pick a trigger, add optional conditions and the actions to execute.
workflows.empty: No automations yet. Create the first one.
workflows.engineNote: Actions run inline after the primary mutation succeeds. Failures are logged but never block the user.

form.name: Name
form.conditionsHint: All conditions must be true for actions to run. Empty = always runs.
form.actionsHint: Executed in order. Failure on one doesn't stop the rest.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.subtitle: Send signed HTTP POST to external systems when internal events fire.
webhooks.securityNote: Each webhook has a rotating secret. Receivers should compute HMAC-SHA256(secret, raw_body) and compare against the X-Backpackers-Signature header (format: sha256=<hex>).
webhooks.secretHint: Use this to verify the X-Backpackers-Signature header on your receiver.
webhooks.rotateConfirm: Rotate the secret? The receiver must switch to the new secret immediately.
webhooks.testSentToast: Test sent. See the deliveries list below.
webhooks.rotatedToast: Secret rotated. Update the receiver with the new value.
webhooks.noDeliveries: No deliveries yet for this webhook.

audit.title: Audit
audit.subtitle: Immutable log of every mutation. Filter by entity, action or user.
audit.count: {count, plural, =0 {No entries} one {1 entry} other {# entries}}
audit.empty: No entries for the current filters.

resendTest.title: Test email (Resend)
resendTest.subtitle: Verify RESEND_API_KEY is configured. The button sends a canary email.
resendTest.statusConfigured: RESEND_API_KEY is set. Ready to send.
resendTest.statusNotConfigured: RESEND_API_KEY is not set. Add it in Vercel → Env Vars and redeploy.
resendTest.step1: Create an account at resend.com and generate an API key.
resendTest.step2: Recommended: add a verified domain (DKIM + SPF).
resendTest.step3: Vercel → Settings → Environment Variables → add RESEND_API_KEY and (optional) RESEND_FROM_EMAIL.
resendTest.step4: Redeploy and come back to test.

templates.title: Templates
templates.subtitle: Reusable snippets for notes and descriptions.

form.name: Name
form.bodyHint: Plain text. No placeholders in this version.

invite.title: Invite user
invite.subtitle: Create an account and assign role, skills and pillar access. The user receives an email to set their password.
invite.sendInviteHint: If checked, Supabase sends an email with a password-setup link. If unchecked, the account is created already confirmed — set the password later via password recovery.

errors.cannotGrantSuperUser: Only super users can grant the super user role.
errors.inviteFailed: Could not create the user. Make sure the email isn't already registered.

dealCopilot.title: Deal copilot
dealCopilot.description: Summary of the current state and 2-3 concrete next actions.
dealCopilot.notConfigured: Copilot disabled. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to enable.

projectCopilot.title: Project copilot
projectCopilot.description: Reads the current task list and suggests the gaps still missing.
projectCopilot.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestion — validate before creating tasks.
projectCopilot.notConfigured: Copilot disabled. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to enable.
